Petition To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Property For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate And For Probate Code Section 859 Damages
Off Calendar Oct-15-2008 Continued To Nov-03-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Noel Lawrence for Dale Marie Isla (Executor/Petitioner) and specially appearing for John Boessenecker.

Ms. Lawrence informed the Court that all matters had settled and presented an endorsed copy of the stipulation asking the Court to sign an order retaining jurisdiction under CCP 664.6. In addition, Ms. Lawrence stated that the Petition To Establish The Estate's Claim of Ownership and the Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief filed by her client were dismissed with prejudice pursuant to paragraph 10 of the stipulation. Furthermore, she stated that the Petition For Spousal Property and the Petition For Family Allowance filed by Mr. Boessenecker's client were dismissed with prejudice pursuant to paragraph 10 of the stipulation. Upon review of the stipulation and order, the Court informed Ms. Lawrence that the order would not be signed as the settlement appeared to involve a trust which was not before the Court and that there was no pending 664.6 motion. The Court informed Ms. Lawrence that the time to file the motion would be in the event that there was a breach of the settlement. Status on filing of the 4 dismissals was set for Novmeber 3, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Wong.

Petition To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Property For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate And For Probate Code Section 859 Damages
Off Calendar Sep-24-2008 Continued To Oct-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Loretta Broussard.

Mr. Boessencker informed the Court that Counsel Noel Lawrence was not able to appear, stated that they agreed to continue the matter and that they were working with a neutral case evaluator. In addition, he stated that they had entered into an outline of the settlement, but that they still needed to resolve some issues. The matter was continued to October 15, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico.

Off Calendar Mar-19-2008 Continued To Jun-18-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Loretta Broussard. Noel Lawrence for Dale Marie Isla.

Noel Lawrence stated that the Inventory and Appraisal on the personal property had been filed and presented an endorsed copy to the Court. In addition, she stated that Mr. Boessenecker and herself were trying to work out ownership of the bank accounts, the 850 petition regarding the deed dispute and that they were hoping to settle the case. The Court continued the matter to June 18, 2008. Mr. Boessenecker requested continuing the Petition for Spousal Property and the Petition For Family Allowance to June 16, 2008, which the Court granted.

Judicial Officer: Judge John Dearman; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: F. Smith; Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Petitioner.

Mr. Boessenecker informed the Court that he received a notice of non opposition from Ms. Lawrence. There being no opposition, the Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Hill.

Petition To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Property For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate And For Probate Code Section 859 Damages
Off Calendar Nov-28-2007 Continued To Feb-20-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Loretta Brousard (Respondent). Noel Lawrence for Dale Isla (Petitioner).

The Court stated that an Inventory and Appraisal of the personal property should be done. Ms. Lawrence reported that she already aranged for an appraiser to come out, but that a date needed to be selected for the inspection. Mr. Boessenecker stated that the community property not being used for his client's day to day living would be turned over, that he would be filing a Petition for Spousal Property and Family Allowance, and that they needed to do discovery. The Court will not make a decision until the Inventory and Appraisal is filed. The matter was continued to February 20, 2008 for status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lee and Deputy Smith.
